evals.prompt: Bring over the config management screen from platform to tenant dashboard
evals.expected_output: DUDA TRANSPLANT mode activates, detects transplant-deny item (config management), selects Strategy 4 (transplant denied), and blocks execution. No code is copied.
expectations: Phase 0 asks user to confirm this is a transplant operation
Config management or UPPER-ONLY or transplant-deny is cited as reason
No code is copied or files created

evals.prompt: The tenant menu list page is showing data from other organizations. Why?
evals.expected_output: DUDA AUDIT mode activates and traces contamination. Determines Type B (multi-tenant) isolation policy leak as root cause. Presents recovery prompt to add tenant identifier (org_id) and includes verification checklist.
expectations: Symptom layer (tenant) and exposed data (other org data) are captured
grep or audit.py based contamination path search is performed
Type B (multi-tenant) or isolation policy leak is determined as root cause
org_id or tenant identifier addition is presented as recovery method
Verification checklist is included in output

evals.prompt: I want to use the LoadingSpinner component from platform in tenant screens too
evals.expected_output: DUDA TRANSPLANT mode activates, analyzes LoadingSpinner, confirms [SHARED] tag, and selects Strategy 1 (direct reference). Provides safe import from packages/ path. No file copying.
expectations: Import from packages/ or shared path is provided
LoadingSpinner is NOT copy-pasted into tenant/ directory

evals.expected_output: DUDA INIT mode activates and runs init.py-based topological flood fill exploration. Generates DUDA_MAP.md and requests user approval.
expectations: init.py or topological flood fill exploration is executed
Leaf files are collected first in topological sort
DUDA_MAP.md is generated or generation is attempted
Ambiguous tag items are separately listed

evals.prompt: Use the useMenuStore from platform in tenant app
evals.expected_output: DUDA TRANSPLANT mode activates, detects unapproved DUDA_MAP state, and blocks at trust gate. Instructs to run 'duda init' or approve the map first.
expectations: Map unapproved state causes low map trust score
duda init or approval procedure is instructed

evals.prompt: The tenant screen is suddenly showing the platform admin menu
evals.expected_output: DUDA AUDIT mode activates and detects Type A (component contamination) root cause. Finds lower layer file importing upper-only component (AdminMenu) and presents removal/fix prompt with verification checklist.
expectations: Type A (component contamination) or UPPER-ONLY import is identified as root cause
Search for upper-path imports in lower layer is performed
UPPER-ONLY import removal is presented as fix
Inference-based analysis is used if actual files are not found

evals.prompt: I want to use the MenuCard component from platform in tenant, but platform-only cost fields must not be visible
evals.expected_output: DUDA TRANSPLANT mode activates, detects UPPER-ONLY dependency (masterConfigStore/rawCostStore), and selects Strategy 2 (adapter branching). Presents mode prop or conditional rendering approach to hide cost fields.
expectations: Upper-only store or cost-related import is detected as UPPER-ONLY
Strategy 1 (direct copy) or Strategy 4 (deny) is NOT selected
Mode prop or conditional rendering method is presented
Upper-only store is NOT directly imported in tenant

evals.prompt: Apply the login logic from platform to tenant
evals.expected_output: DUDA_MAP missing is detected and TRANSPLANT is blocked. Instructs to run 'duda init' first.
expectations: duda init or initialization is instructed first
No transplant analysis is performed without map

evals.expected_output: DUDA SCAN mode activates (lite mode), analyzes the single file without requiring DUDA_MAP, and outputs layer tag, risk level, and import analysis.
expectations: All imports in the file are listed and tagged
Quick recommendation is given (safe to use / needs adapter / deny)

evals.prompt: Order service is reading directly from inventory database instead of going through the API
evals.expected_output: DUDA AUDIT mode activates, detects Type D (microservice boundary violation). Identifies direct DB access across service boundary and provides API-based fix with verification checklist.
expectations: Type D (microservice) boundary violation is identified
Direct DB access across service boundary is flagged

evals.prompt: Can I use the shared Button component from packages/ui in my tenant app?
evals.expected_output: DUDA TRANSPLANT mode activates, confirms the component is in packages/ (shared), assigns [SHARED] tag, and permits with Strategy 1. Minimal friction for safe operations.
expectations: packages/ path is recognized as shared
Trust score is high due to packages/ being inherently shared

evals.prompt: Apps in our monorepo are importing directly from each other instead of going through packages
evals.expected_output: DUDA AUDIT mode activates, detects Type C (monorepo boundary violation). Identifies cross-app imports and recommends extracting to packages/.

evals.prompt: Initialize the isolation map for this project
evals.expected_output: DUDA INIT mode activates via natural language trigger (not explicit 'duda init'). Runs the same topological exploration and DUDA_MAP generation.
expectations: INIT mode activates from natural language
init.py or topological exploration is executed

evals.prompt: Migrate the dashboard analytics module from the platform app to the tenant app. It uses dynamic imports based on user role.
evals.expected_output: DUDA TRANSPLANT mode activates, detects [UNVERIFIABLE] tags due to dynamic imports, and either blocks or requires manual verification before proceeding.
expectations: Manual verification is required before proceeding
Trust score is reduced due to unverifiable items
Resolution steps for converting dynamic to static imports are suggested

evals.prompt: Tenant A is seeing tenant B's cached menu data
evals.expected_output: DUDA AUDIT mode activates, detects Type B (shared cache without tenant key). Identifies cache key missing org_id and provides fix.
expectations: Type B (multi-tenant) cache contamination is identified
Missing tenant identifier in cache key is flagged
Fix includes adding org_id to cache key

evals.prompt: I need to use platform's error handling middleware in the tenant service
evals.expected_output: DUDA TRANSPLANT mode activates, analyzes middleware dependencies, determines it likely contains platform-specific logic, and recommends appropriate strategy (adapter or rebuild).
expectations: Platform-specific vs shared logic is distinguished
Appropriate strategy (2 or 3) is selected based on analysis
No direct copy of middleware is allowed
